arms_mandatory_baselines: kernel-v0 + molecules-v0 + wn31-aligned bulk projection (pinned static corpus)

dependent_vars.definition: records breaching a grammar cap OR >1.5x clause growth when relational content is inlined (union, per record)
dependent_vars.name: n_breached_either

endpoints.test: deterministic bulk-projection count vs the pre-registered 10% bound (NF6); count-based fork criterion, carries no statistical test (P1 common rules)

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: P1 §4b row HS2–HS8 (verbatim): Measured range: R0 — no host model. Reasonable extrapolation envelope: Model-scale-free (properties of the kernel formalism); the relevant axis is KERNEL size: verdicts on 54–10³ records re-checked at 10⁴–10⁵ during bulk authoring (G6/G7 re-run as regression). Literature anchor + licensing assumption: Formal-fragment properties don't vary with observer scale; only corpus composition shifts them.
kill_criterion_verbatim: P1 §4 HS7 statement + kill (verbatim): '(HS7) inlining relational content forces cap violations or >1.5× clause growth on >10% of records at bulk scale ⇒ apply-clauses (kot-ast/2) win; else defer.' Kill (P1 §8): 'cap violations/1.5× growth >10% ⇒ kot-ast/2.' Kill criteria are the numeric bounds from NF6, verbatim. FAIL here means the fork selects kot-ast/2 (a design selection, not a programme kill).

corpus_hashes._recipe: sha256 over the sorted concatenation of '<relpath>:<file-sha256>\n' lines for every file under data/<corpus>/

title: G7 — apply-clause cap/growth bulk projection count (HS7 / NF6; deterministic count, no test)
